hypothesis: Under matched mean offered work with heavy-tailed completion lengths, class-estimated token-work admission will reduce short-request p95 latency and active-work oversubscription relative to a 16-request concurrency limit, while the advantage will shrink under a fixed-length negative control.
claimBoundary: The evidence supports operating characteristics of the declared processor-sharing simulation and class-level token estimates. It does not establish GPU throughput, model quality, scheduler behavior, prefill/decode interference, or a universal token budget for production systems.

baselines: Fixed 16-request concurrent-admission limit under identical arrivals and output-token draws
Oracle token-work budget as a positive-control upper bound
controls: Fixed 900-token completion workload as a negative control for output-length heterogeneity
Half-scale and double-scale estimate ablations to expose calibration sensitivity
Shortest-estimate-first queue ablation to isolate FIFO head-of-line blocking
